zone_6b_notes: Hardy in Zone 6b. Plant two varieties for cross-pollination (Bartlett and Moonglow are a classic Missouri pairing). Fire blight is pear's biggest threat in Missouri humidity — choose fire-blight resistant varieties like Moonglow, Harrow Sweet, or Harrow Delight. Avoid Bradford pear (ornamental, invasive). European pears should be harvested slightly underripe and allowed to ripen indoors at room temperature — fruit left to ripen on the tree turns gritty. Asian pears ripen on tree. Pears are longer-lived than peaches; a well-sited pear can produce 50+ years.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant dormant bare-root trees March to early April

water: 1 inch/week during establishment; moderate once established
